title: Microterangis coursiana
intro: Microterangis coursiana, also known as the Coursiana orchid, is a species of orchid native to Madagascar. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including the potential to lower blood pressure and reduce inflammation, which may contribute to the alleviation of symptoms associated with conditions such as hypertension and arthritis. The bioactive constituents of Microterangis coursiana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Microterangis coursiana is often prepared as a tea or a tincture, and is used to support cardiovascular health and reduce oxidative stress.
